rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $668.00 U.S. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Answers To First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Docs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 19, 2014, Line 1, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA Motion To Compel Answers To First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The People of the State of California's (the "People's") Motion to Compel Answers to Petitioner's First Set of Demand [sic] for Production and Inspection of Documents, Petitioners First Set of Special Interrogatories, Judicial Council Form Interrogatories, is neither tentatively GRANTED nor DENIED. Rather, all parties to appear. Since the People failed to notice, failed to state in the caption, failed to persuasively argue in memorandum, and failed to set out support in declaration in support of sanctions, therefore sanctions of any and all sort are DENIED. The People shall be prepared to present evidence that the People timely filed copies of the associated discovery demands with the clerk of court putively attached to Assistant District Attorney Raymond Fong's Declaration of October 30, 2014. Using contact information available, the People shall notify Michael Polk of this tentative and of the date, time, and location of the instant hearing. At hearing, the People shall submit a proposed order setting out the relief it seeks, describing efforts made to notify Mr. Polk of this hearing, and integrating the issues discussed in this tentative ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMBILE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. REYNALDO S CANTERO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion To Amend Complaint By Filing Doe Amendment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 19, 2014, Line 10, PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMBILE INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Amend Complaint By Filing Doe Amendment. Continued to December 2, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than November 19, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JOY B VS. ANDREW S.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Motion To Relieve Pltff From A Stipulation And For A Protective Order Or, In The Alternative, To Compel Deft'S Further Responses To Special Interrogs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 19, 2014, Line 10, PLAINTIFF JOY B Motion To Relieve Plaintiff From A Stipulation And For A Protective Order Or, In The Alternative, To Compel Defendant's Further Responses To Special Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Joy B's ("Joy's") Motion to Relieve Plaintiff from a Stipulation and for a Protective Order is DENIED without prejudice. Joy's Notice of Motion is captioned as follows: "Plaintiff's Motion and Notice of Motion to Relieve Plaintiff from a Stipulation and for a Protective Order or, in the Alternative, to Compel Defendant's Further Responses to Special Interrogatories." From this caption page it appears that Joy seeks a remedy or a remedy in the alternative related to the issuance of a Protective Order and involving the voiding of an earlier-entered Stipulation. But the text of Joy's notice fails to seek relief from a stipulation, fails to seek a protective order, and fails to articulate grounds for either relief from a stipulation or entry of a protective order. See Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 1010 ("Notices must be in writing, and . . . must state when, and the grounds upon which it will be made, and the papers, if any, upon which it is to be based."). As such the notice is infirm, and this Court is without authority to adjudicate those unnoticed issues. As the prevailing party, Defendant Andrew Sause shall prepare a form of the order consistent with this tentative ruling and shall bring a copy of the proposed order to the hearing, if any. In the absence of a hearing, the prevailing party shall timely comply with Cal. R. Ct. 3.1312. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: Fernandez v. Regents of the University of California San Francisco et al.
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 19, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT WALGREEN CO.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer is overruled in its entirety. Plaintiff is not required to allege the date of injury or negligent act in order to state a claim for either cause of action. Further, the absence of either date does not render the First Amended Complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
